title: Smoky Tangy Venezuelan Baby Squid Stew
description: A dense, one‑pot Venezuelan stew bursting with smoky flavor from tandoor‑charred baby squid and bright tang from lime and cilantro.

ingredients.name: baby squid (calamares)
ingredients.notes: fresh or frozen, thawed, rinse, pat dry

instructions: Preheat a tandoor or charcoal grill to medium‑high heat; char the baby squid for about 2 minutes per side until slightly blackened and smoky. Remove and set aside.
Heat olive oil in a large pot over medium heat. Add diced shallots and cook until translucent, about 3–4 minutes.
Add minced garlic and sauté for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Stir in tomato paste and cook for 1 minute, then add diced tomatoes, smoked paprika, cumin, salt, and black pepper. Simmer the sauce for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Return the charred baby squid to the pot. Pour in lime juice and add 2 cups of water or fish stock.
Bring the mixture to a gentle boil, then reduce heat and simmer uncovered for 10 minutes, ensuring the squid reaches an internal temperature of at least 145°F.
Stir in chopped cilantro, taste and adjust seasoning if needed.
Serve hot, accompanied by fresh lime wedges on the side.
